Fig. 3. Qualitative results of ablation studies.

Fig. 2. Qualitative comparison with peer methods. The video (a) and (e) denote source video while the other video are edited video from (b) TokenFlow, (f) MiniMax-Remover, (c)&(g) AnyV2V, (d)&(h) our model.

Fig. 1. The overview of our proposed methods. Utilizing the tailored framework, which incorporates EIAM, SNIS and NGM, source video S can be transformed into target video T according to user instruction I. G-SAM-2 denotes Grounded-SAM-2. At timestep 𝑡, we introduce the SNIS to get a denoised starting state 𝑧𝑡 . During the period 𝑖 ∈ [𝛼𝑇, 𝛽𝑇], we introduce the NGM to maintain the background and optimize the generated video by leveraging video priors.
